verses.text: Elisha sends a young prophet to Ramoth-gilead to pour oil on Jehu's head and flee. The long-promised judgment on Ahab's house begins quietly, with a secret anointing. God sets His instruments in place before the storm of His justice breaks.

Elisha sends a young prophet to Ramoth-gilead to pour oil on Jehu's head and flee. The long-promised judgment on Ahab's house begins quietly, with a secret anointing. God sets His instruments in place before the storm of His justice breaks.

Elisha sends a young prophet to Ramoth-gilead to pour oil on Jehu's head and flee. The long-promised judgment on Ahab's house begins quietly, with a secret anointing. God sets His instruments in place before the storm of His justice breaks.

The young man anoints Jehu king over Israel and charges him to strike the house of Ahab, to avenge the blood of God's prophets shed by Jezebel. The commission is one of judgment. God appoints an executioner for the crimes long crying out against that house.

The young man anoints Jehu king over Israel and charges him to strike the house of Ahab, to avenge the blood of God's prophets shed by Jezebel. The commission is one of judgment. God appoints an executioner for the crimes long crying out against that house.

The young man anoints Jehu king over Israel and charges him to strike the house of Ahab, to avenge the blood of God's prophets shed by Jezebel. The commission is one of judgment. God appoints an executioner for the crimes long crying out against that house.

The young man anoints Jehu king over Israel and charges him to strike the house of Ahab, to avenge the blood of God's prophets shed by Jezebel. The commission is one of judgment. God appoints an executioner for the crimes long crying out against that house.

The whole house of Ahab is to perish, and dogs shall eat Jezebel in Jezreel, with none to bury her — then the prophet flees. The sentence Elijah spoke is now set in motion. God forgets no shed innocent blood; its reckoning comes in His time.

The whole house of Ahab is to perish, and dogs shall eat Jezebel in Jezreel, with none to bury her — then the prophet flees. The sentence Elijah spoke is now set in motion. God forgets no shed innocent blood; its reckoning comes in His time.

The whole house of Ahab is to perish, and dogs shall eat Jezebel in Jezreel, with none to bury her — then the prophet flees. The sentence Elijah spoke is now set in motion. God forgets no shed innocent blood; its reckoning comes in His time.

Jehu's fellow captains press him about the 'mad fellow,' and when they hear, they spread their garments under him and blow the trumpet: 'Jehu is king.' The army rallies at once. When God moves to act, He swiftly gathers the men and means to carry it out.

Jehu's fellow captains press him about the 'mad fellow,' and when they hear, they spread their garments under him and blow the trumpet: 'Jehu is king.' The army rallies at once. When God moves to act, He swiftly gathers the men and means to carry it out.

Jehu's fellow captains press him about the 'mad fellow,' and when they hear, they spread their garments under him and blow the trumpet: 'Jehu is king.' The army rallies at once. When God moves to act, He swiftly gathers the men and means to carry it out.

Jehu drives at once for Jezreel, where wounded Joram lies and Ahaziah is visiting, and orders that none leave to warn them. Speed and secrecy serve the judgment. The two evil kings are gathered, unsuspecting, to the place of reckoning.

Jehu drives at once for Jezreel, where wounded Joram lies and Ahaziah is visiting, and orders that none leave to warn them. Speed and secrecy serve the judgment. The two evil kings are gathered, unsuspecting, to the place of reckoning.

Jehu drives at once for Jezreel, where wounded Joram lies and Ahaziah is visiting, and orders that none leave to warn them. Speed and secrecy serve the judgment. The two evil kings are gathered, unsuspecting, to the place of reckoning.

A watchman reports a company approaching, and two messengers sent out do not return; at last he says, 'the driving is like the driving of Jehu, for he driveth furiously.' The relentless approach of judgment is unmistakable. God's reckoning comes on without pause.

A watchman reports a company approaching, and two messengers sent out do not return; at last he says, 'the driving is like the driving of Jehu, for he driveth furiously.' The relentless approach of judgment is unmistakable. God's reckoning comes on without pause.

A watchman reports a company approaching, and two messengers sent out do not return; at last he says, 'the driving is like the driving of Jehu, for he driveth furiously.' The relentless approach of judgment is unmistakable. God's reckoning comes on without pause.

Joram rides out to meet Jehu in Naboth's field and cries 'Is it peace?' — Jehu answers, what peace, while Jezebel's whoredoms abound? and shoots him through the heart. The king dies on the ground stolen from Naboth. Sin's penalty falls on the very plot it seized.

Joram rides out to meet Jehu in Naboth's field and cries 'Is it peace?' — Jehu answers, what peace, while Jezebel's whoredoms abound? and shoots him through the heart. The king dies on the ground stolen from Naboth. Sin's penalty falls on the very plot it seized.

Joram rides out to meet Jehu in Naboth's field and cries 'Is it peace?' — Jehu answers, what peace, while Jezebel's whoredoms abound? and shoots him through the heart. The king dies on the ground stolen from Naboth. Sin's penalty falls on the very plot it seized.

Jehu has Joram's body cast into Naboth's field, recalling the LORD's word against Ahab there. The murdered man's vineyard becomes the murderer's son's grave. God repays the wrong on the exact ground where it was done; His justice is precise.

Jehu has Joram's body cast into Naboth's field, recalling the LORD's word against Ahab there. The murdered man's vineyard becomes the murderer's son's grave. God repays the wrong on the exact ground where it was done; His justice is precise.

Ahaziah of Judah flees but is struck down and dies at Megiddo, and is carried to Jerusalem for burial. The Judean king, entangled with Ahab's house, shares its judgment. Bad company in life draws us into its ruin; we reap where we have sown our alliances.

Ahaziah of Judah flees but is struck down and dies at Megiddo, and is carried to Jerusalem for burial. The Judean king, entangled with Ahab's house, shares its judgment. Bad company in life draws us into its ruin; we reap where we have sown our alliances.

Jezebel, hearing Jehu has come, paints her eyes, adorns her head, and taunts him from a window as a king-killer. Defiant to the last, she meets doom dressed for a throne. Hardened pride flaunts itself even at the very edge of the grave.

Jezebel, hearing Jehu has come, paints her eyes, adorns her head, and taunts him from a window as a king-killer. Defiant to the last, she meets doom dressed for a throne. Hardened pride flaunts itself even at the very edge of the grave.

Jehu calls for allies at the window; eunuchs throw her down, her blood spatters the wall, and the horses trample her. He goes in to eat, then says, bury this cursed woman, a king's daughter. The mighty queen is suddenly nothing, crushed underfoot.

Jehu calls for allies at the window; eunuchs throw her down, her blood spatters the wall, and the horses trample her. He goes in to eat, then says, bury this cursed woman, a king's daughter. The mighty queen is suddenly nothing, crushed underfoot.

Jehu calls for allies at the window; eunuchs throw her down, her blood spatters the wall, and the horses trample her. He goes in to eat, then says, bury this cursed woman, a king's daughter. The mighty queen is suddenly nothing, crushed underfoot.

They find only her skull, feet, and hands — dogs have eaten the rest, exactly as Elijah foretold: her flesh as dung on the field, none able to say 'this is Jezebel.' The fearful word is fulfilled to the letter. God's judgment, long delayed, comes at last in full.
